criteria.name: seeded_transactions_visible_on_load
criteria.description: On load, the transactions list shows at least 8 seeded rows with date, payee, category, account, and amount visible, spanning enough expense categories (roughly 8-10) that the breakdown legend and pie are visibly multi-category

criteria.name: created_transaction_appears_in_list
criteria.description: After creating a transaction with payee Ada Books and a valid amount matching the field contract, the text Ada Books is visible in the transactions list

criteria.name: three_creates_add_exactly_three_rows
criteria.description: After adding 3 new valid transactions on top of the seed set, the list shows exactly 3 more rows than the initial seeded count

criteria.name: expense_create_updates_kpi
criteria.description: After creating an expense transaction, Total Expenses or Net KPI updates to reflect the new amount

criteria.name: edited_payee_replaces_old_value
criteria.description: After editing a payee from the seeded value to Evening Market, the text Evening Market is visible and the previous payee is not

criteria.name: deleted_transaction_removed_from_list
criteria.description: After deleting a transaction, that payee is no longer visible in the transactions list

criteria.name: empty_state_after_deleting_all
criteria.description: After deleting all transactions, the list region shows an empty state message with no transaction rows remaining, and the summary metric cards and transaction summary strip reflect zero/empty

criteria.name: invalid_create_blocked_with_messages
criteria.description: After submitting create with an empty payee or missing amount, no row is added, the transactions count is unchanged, and inline validation messages naming the offending fields are visible

criteria.name: category_filter_narrows_list
criteria.description: After filtering transactions by a category (with mixed categories present), only matching rows remain visible

criteria.name: chart_tab_switch_stays_in_page
criteria.description: After switching between Breakdown and Trends chart tabs, the chart panel swaps without full page navigation

criteria.name: chart_and_list_modes_coexist
criteria.description: After focusing the transactions list versus the chart panel (list vs chart interaction modes), both remain usable without leaving the page

criteria.name: hover_feedback_on_interactive_chrome
criteria.description: When hovering sidebar items, transaction rows, or primary buttons, a visible hover wash or shadow change appears

criteria.name: bulk_action_updates_rows_and_aggregates
criteria.description: After selecting multiple transaction rows and running bulk categorize or bulk delete, the selected rows update category together or disappear from the list and KPIs/charts reflect the change

criteria.name: sankey_flow_structure_and_legend
criteria.description: In Breakdown view, the sankey-style flow shows income-source nodes feeding an Income hub that splits into a Net Income node and expense-category nodes, with node labels showing amounts and a per-category legend listing each expense category's amount and share percentage

criteria.name: summary_strip_tracks_collection
criteria.description: The transaction summary strip (total count, largest transaction, average transaction, total income, first/last transaction dates) reflects the current collection and updates after a transaction is added or deleted

criteria.name: kpi_cards_derive_from_collection
criteria.description: Summary metric cards (Total Income, Total Expenses, Net Income, Savings Rate) derive from the transactions collection, with Net Income equal to income minus expenses and Savings Rate equal to net divided by income as a percentage, and they update when a transaction is created, edited, or deleted

criteria.name: trends_doughnut_legend_and_tooltip
criteria.description: Trends view renders a doughnut of expense categories with a legend showing each category's amount and percentage of total expenses, and hovering a slice reveals a tooltip with that amount and percentage

criteria.name: filters_recompute_and_show_empty_state
criteria.description: Category and income-vs-expense filters recompute the visible list from the shared collection, and a filter matching nothing shows an empty state message instead of a blank table

criteria.name: create_flow_multi_surface_chain
criteria.description: Starting from the seeded state: note the table row count and the four summary metric card values, then create a valid transaction via the form. Confirm the table gains exactly one row (count delta measured immediately around the action), the four summary metric cards recompute to include its amount, the transaction summary strip updates its count and average, and switching to the Breakdown or Trends chart shows that transaction's category reflected in the node labels, slices, and legend amounts — all without a page reload

criteria.name: edit_amount_propagates_to_both_legends
criteria.description: After editing an existing transaction's amount via the UI, that same row shows the new amount in the table, the summary metric cards change by exactly the difference, and the matching category's amount and percentage update in both the pie legend and the sankey flow legend without a page reload

criteria.name: delete_flow_clears_row_selection_and_aggregates
criteria.description: After selecting a transaction row and then deleting that transaction: its row disappears from the table and from the current selection, the summary strip count decreases by exactly one, and its amount is dropped from the summary metric cards and from every chart legend

criteria.name: filter_clear_restores_full_list_exactly
criteria.description: Applying the category filter narrows the visible table rows to that category and clearing the filter restores the full list exactly (same rows and count as before filtering); the income vs expense filter narrows and restores the same way

criteria.name: double_submit_creates_exactly_one_row
criteria.description: Double-activating the create form's submit control in quick succession creates exactly one transaction: the row count increases by one and exactly one new row appears

criteria.name: ledger_sidebar_shell_with_inert_nav
criteria.description: The sidebar shows a branded Ledger nav of roughly a dozen items (Dashboard, Accounts, Transactions, Cash Flow, Reports, Budget, Recurring, Goals, Investments, Forecasting, Advice) with Reports as the active item, a Shared demo report eyebrow, Demo mode/Sample data chips, notification and settings icons, and an Alex Rivera profile; clicking any other nav item is an inert demo control that does not navigate away from the reports page

criteria.name: inert_chrome_and_tab_switch_fire_toasts
criteria.description: Clicking inert chrome actions (Filters, Date, Save, Sort, Columns) and switching chart tabs each fire a demo toast confirming the action, while real create, edit, delete, export, and import perform actual state changes rather than being replaced by a toast

criteria.name: charts_redraw_on_filter_change
criteria.description: After applying the category or income-vs-expense filter, the sankey node labels, pie slices, legends, and summary tiles redraw to the new filtered totals without a page reload, and the redrawn values meaningfully differ from the unfiltered ones

criteria.name: form_submit_disabled_until_valid
criteria.description: The transaction create/edit form presents date, payee, category, account, amount, and optional status fields; the submit control stays disabled until every required field is valid against the field contract, and each invalid field shows an inline message naming that field before submit

criteria.name: income_rows_styled_positive
criteria.description: In the transactions list, income amounts render with a leading + and a distinct positive (green) color while expense amounts render neutral

criteria.name: field_contract_rejects_zero_and_cross_field
criteria.description: Submitting create with amount 0, or with category Salary and a negative amount, adds no row; inline validation messages name the offending fields and the transactions count is unchanged

criteria.name: date_range_and_payee_search_narrow_list
criteria.description: Applying a date-range filter narrows visible rows to dates inside the inclusive range, and typing a payee search query narrows rows to case-insensitive substring matches; clearing each control restores the prior wider list

criteria.name: burn_rate_panel_tracks_ceiling_and_overage
criteria.description: The Burn rate panel shows daily expense totals for the current calendar month against an editable monthly ceiling and a projected month-end spend; lowering the ceiling below the projection shows a visible over-burn treatment that names the overage, and raising it above clears the treatment

criteria.name: command_palette_runs_declared_commands
criteria.description: Pressing Cmd+K or Ctrl+K opens a command palette listing at least New transaction, Switch to Breakdown, Switch to Trends, Export report, Import CSV, Focus payee search, and Clear filters; choosing Switch to Trends activates the Trends panel and choosing Export report opens the export drawer

criteria.name: export_report_json_and_markdown_reflect_session
criteria.description: After creating transactions with payees Ada Books and Evening Market, Export report shows a JSON tab whose transactions array contains both payees and whose totals.count equals the live summary strip count, and a Markdown tab titled Expense Breakdown Report with Totals and Category breakdown sections; Download JSON and Download Markdown offer expense-breakdown-report.json and expense-breakdown-report.md; Copy shows a Copied confirmation

criteria.name: import_csv_diagnostic_commits_valid_rows
criteria.description: Import CSV accepts a header date,payee,category,account,amount,status; invalid rows appear in a mismatch list naming the row number and offending field; committing valid rows with Replace all updates the table, KPIs, charts, and burn rate, and a subsequent export reflects the committed rows

criteria.name: command_palette_filter_and_escape
criteria.description: With the command palette open, typing filters the command list by title; Escape closes the palette without performing any action; choosing a command (for example New transaction or Clear filters) closes the palette and performs that action through the same handlers as the visible controls

criteria.name: export_mirrors_burnrate_and_filters
criteria.description: The Export report JSON includes a filters object mirroring the active category, type, date-range, and payee controls and a burnRate object (ceiling, monthToDate, projectedMonthEnd, over) mirroring the Burn rate panel; the Markdown tab includes a Burn rate section naming ceiling, month-to-date, projected month-end, and over status

criteria.name: import_append_mode_adds_rows
criteria.description: Import CSV with the Append mode commits valid rows on top of the existing collection: the table row count increases by exactly the number of committed valid rows and the KPIs, charts, burn rate, and a subsequent export include both the prior and appended transactions

criteria.name: controls_are_keyboard_accessible
criteria.description: Sidebar items, chrome actions, chart tabs, table controls, form fields, Export report, Import CSV, Burn rate ceiling, and command palette items are reachable and operable with the keyboard alone

criteria.name: modals_manage_focus
criteria.description: Create/edit dialog, export drawer, and command palette move focus inside on open, trap focus while open, close on Escape, and return focus to the invoking control

criteria.name: images_and_icons_have_alt_text
criteria.description: Meaningful icons and images in the sidebar and chrome expose accessible names or alt text

criteria.name: feedback_uses_live_regions
criteria.description: Validation messages, toasts, and Copied confirmations are announced via a polite live region as well as shown visually

criteria.name: forms_have_explicit_labels
criteria.description: Transaction create/edit fields (date, payee, category, account, amount, status) and the burn-rate ceiling input have programmatically associated labels

criteria.name: headings_follow_logical_order
criteria.description: Page and panel headings follow a logical order without skipping levels in the reports workspace

criteria.name: landmark_navigation_is_present
criteria.description: Landmark regions such as navigation and main are present so the sidebar and reports column are distinguishable

criteria.name: text_and_controls_have_contrast
criteria.description: Text and controls in the soft mint finance UI meet readable contrast against their backgrounds

criteria.name: semantic_html_roles_are_used
criteria.description: Interactive controls use semantic roles (button, dialog/complementary for overlays, navigation for the sidebar)

criteria.name: reduced_motion_is_respected
criteria.description: With prefers-reduced-motion set, entry/exit animations are removed while every feature stays reachable

criteria.name: charts_and_over_burn_have_text_alternatives
criteria.description: Chart legends list every expense category with amount and percentage as text; over-burn status names the projected overage in text, not color alone

criteria.name: empty_state_is_present
criteria.description: After deleting all transactions or filtering to zero matches, the list region shows an empty state message rather than a blank table

criteria.name: forms_validate_inline
criteria.description: Create/edit forms show inline per-field validation for empty payee, missing amount, zero amount, non-ISO date, and cross-field Salary-with-negative before submit

criteria.name: errors_are_actionable
criteria.description: Validation and import mismatch messages name the offending field or row number rather than only saying Invalid

criteria.name: actions_show_confirmation
criteria.description: Successful Copy shows a Copied confirmation, and demo chrome actions show a toast confirmation

criteria.name: async_work_shows_loading_state
criteria.description: Where import commit or export compile takes noticeable time, a brief busy/progress indicator is visible rather than a frozen blank control

criteria.name: destructive_actions_support_undo_or_cancel
criteria.description: Delete and bulk delete can be cancelled from a confirmation step, or dismissed before commit, so accidental activation does not silently wipe rows without a chance to abort

criteria.name: double_submit_creates_one_row
criteria.description: Double-activating a valid create submit creates exactly one transaction

criteria.name: empty_export_still_valid
criteria.description: With an empty collection, Export report still produces JSON with version 1, zero totals, empty transactions, and Markdown titled Expense Breakdown Report; Copy still shows Copied

criteria.name: import_rejects_bad_header_and_invalid_rows
criteria.description: Import CSV with a bad header or only invalid rows shows mismatch/error feedback and leaves the collection unchanged; mixed rows can commit only valid ones via Commit valid rows

criteria.name: invalid_date_range_and_ceiling_rejected
criteria.description: A date range with end before start shows an inline message and does not apply; a zero or negative burn-rate ceiling is rejected with an inline message naming the ceiling field

criteria.name: hover_washes_and_pointer_cursors
criteria.description: Hovering (with the real pointer) sidebar/nav items and action controls shows a pointer cursor and a hover wash, transaction rows take a light mint wash on hover, and chart sectors keep a pointer cursor

criteria.name: mode_switch_keeps_page_alive
criteria.description: Chart/list mode switches update without full reload and keep hover feedback

criteria.name: chart_tab_pill_toggle_swaps_in_place
criteria.description: Clicking the Breakdown / Trends pill toggle (via the real UI control) swaps the chart panels in place without a page reload, and the active pill gains visibly stronger weight

criteria.name: row_create_and_delete_animate
criteria.description: When a transaction is created or deleted through the real UI controls (not a state shortcut), the new row animates into the table and the deleted row animates out rather than snapping

criteria.name: bulk_delete_rows_animate_out
criteria.description: Bulk deleting several selected rows through the real UI control animates each removed row out rather than snapping the list to its new state

criteria.name: toast_enter_hold_exit_cycle
criteria.description: A demo toast enters from below with a short ease, holds briefly (roughly 1.5s), then exits; toasts fire both on chart-tab switches and on clicking inert chrome actions; Copied confirmation follows the same toast language

criteria.name: pie_slice_hover_tooltip_and_lift
criteria.description: Hovering a Trends pie slice with the real pointer reveals a tooltip showing that category's amount and percentage, and the hovered slice may lift or offset slightly

criteria.name: export_drawer_and_palette_animate_in
criteria.description: Opening the export drawer or command palette through the real UI control uses a short opacity/slide enter transition rather than snapping fully open

criteria.name: spacing_and_sizing_follow_scale
criteria.description: Spacing and sizing among sidebar, metric cards, chart card, and table follow a consistent scale matching the reference screenshots' dense reports layout

criteria.name: typography_matches_spec
criteria.description: Typography hierarchy for page title, metric labels, and table headers matches the soft mint Ledger reports reference treatment

criteria.name: layout_matches_reference
criteria.description: Desktop layout matches the reference: left sidebar with Ledger nav, four statistic cards, chart card with Breakdown/Trends, transactions table, and summary strip

criteria.name: specified_state_changes_animate
criteria.description: Specified state changes from the reference experience (chart tab swap, row add/remove, toast) animate rather than hard-cutting

criteria.name: responsive_behavior_matches_reference
criteria.description: Responsive stacking of the main column at narrow widths matches the reference screenshots' content order expectations

criteria.name: control_styling_matches_spec
criteria.description: Chrome action buttons, chart pills, and table controls match the soft mint control styling of the reference

criteria.name: typography_has_clear_hierarchy
criteria.description: Metric values, section labels, and table body text show a clear size/weight hierarchy as in the reference

criteria.name: component_states_match_spec
criteria.description: Buttons and inputs show distinct default, hover, focus, disabled, and error treatments consistent with the reference chrome

criteria.name: surface_treatments_match_spec
criteria.description: Light page/sidebar wash, teal/mint accents, and card surfaces match the soft mint finance reference look

criteria.name: microinteractions_match_spec
criteria.description: Hover washes on nav/rows and toast enter/exit match the reference microinteraction language

criteria.name: reports_workspace_density
criteria.description: The reports workspace reads as a dense finance tool surface (sidebar, metric cards, chart card, burn-rate panel, table, summary strip) rather than a marketing landing

criteria.name: empty_state_visually_present
criteria.description: When the transactions list is empty, a visible empty-state treatment occupies the list region

criteria.name: income_positive_expense_neutral_multi_hue_charts
criteria.description: Income amounts read as positive (green / leading +), expenses neutral, and Breakdown/Trends charts use multi-hue category fills

criteria.name: soft_mint_palette_and_ledger_mark
criteria.description: The UI uses a soft mint finance palette with brand teal (#0F3D3E) and accent mint (#7BC4B8), and a Ledger mark appears in the sidebar

criteria.name: sidebar_plus_main_column_structure
criteria.description: Layout is a persistent left sidebar plus a main column containing chrome actions, four statistic cards, chart card, burn-rate panel, transactions table, and summary strip

criteria.name: sankey_bars_curved_links_doughnut_legend
criteria.description: Breakdown sankey renders colored bars with curved proportional links and node amount labels; Trends renders a doughnut with a category legend

criteria.name: single_consistent_icon_set
criteria.description: One icon set is used consistently across the sidebar, chrome actions, and table controls with no mixed icon styles

criteria.name: sidebar_persistent_at_desktop_widths
criteria.description: At widths of 1024 pixels and above, the left sidebar stays persistently visible beside the main reports column

criteria.name: narrow_width_stacking_without_overflow
criteria.description: At narrower widths the main column stacks vertically with no horizontal scrolling and no clipped content

criteria.name: export_coachmarks_beyond_spec
criteria.description: Optional: guided first-run coachmarks highlight Export report and the burn-rate panel beyond the required chrome

criteria.name: printable_markdown_stylesheet
criteria.description: Optional: a printable stylesheet improves the Markdown report preview for paper/PDF output beyond the required drawer

criteria.name: palette_keyboard_shortcut_footer
criteria.description: Optional: denser keyboard shortcuts are listed inside the command palette footer beyond the required command list

criteria.name: enhanced_burn_rate_storytelling
criteria.description: Optional: burn-rate projection storytelling (annotations or day callouts) adds clarity beyond the required bars and overage text

criteria.name: delightful_export_microinteractions
criteria.description: Optional: export download or copy offers a delightful microinteraction beyond the required Copied toast

criteria.name: advanced_filter_chips_ux
criteria.description: Optional: removable filter chips summarize category, type, date range, and payee search beyond the required controls

criteria.name: polished_ledger_brand_moments
criteria.description: Optional: polished Ledger brand moments (mark animation or report title treatment) exceed the required sidebar mark

criteria.name: import_diagnostic_row_preview
criteria.description: Optional: import diagnostic offers a richer per-cell preview or fix affordance beyond the required mismatch list

criteria.name: sankey_explore_depth
criteria.description: Optional: sankey node focus or category drill-down explores flows beyond the required structure and legend

criteria.name: competition_level_finance_polish
criteria.description: Optional: overall finance-tool polish visibly exceeds the specification in ways a real operator would notice

criteria.name: innovation_catchall
criteria.description: The app exhibits a significant, browser-observable innovative enhancement beyond the specification that is NOT covered by any other criterion in this file. Cite the concrete evidence (element, interaction, page state). If nothing beyond the covered optional items stands out, answer no.

criteria.name: headings_use_consistent_capitalization
criteria.description: Where the app renders headings, buttons, and nav labels, one consistent capitalization convention is used throughout

criteria.name: actions_use_specific_labels
criteria.description: Where the app renders export and import actions, labels are specific such as Export report, Import CSV, Download JSON, Download Markdown, Copy, and Commit valid rows

criteria.name: errors_name_problem_and_fix
criteria.description: Where the app renders validation or import mismatch errors, messages name the offending field or row and what to fix

criteria.name: empty_states_explain_next_step
criteria.description: Where the app renders an empty transactions region, copy explains what belongs there and how to add it

criteria.name: body_copy_is_well_written
criteria.description: Where the app renders body copy in panels, toasts, and empty states, wording is clear and free of filler

criteria.name: terminology_is_consistent
criteria.description: Where the app names transactions, categories, burn rate, and export report concepts, the same terms are used across chrome and panels

criteria.name: numbers_dates_and_units_are_consistent
criteria.description: Where the app renders currency amounts and dates, formatting is consistent across cards, table, legends, burn-rate panel, and export preview

criteria.name: success_messages_are_specific
criteria.description: Where the app renders success toasts or Copied confirmations, the message names the action confirmed

criteria.name: create_flow_updates_all_surfaces
criteria.description: After submitting a valid new transaction, the table gains exactly one row, the four summary cards recompute, the summary strip count increases by one, and the active chart panel reflects the new category aggregate without a page reload

criteria.name: invalid_create_shows_inline_validation
criteria.description: Submitting create with an empty payee or missing amount shows inline validation naming the offending field and adds no row

criteria.name: edit_flow_updates_related_displays
criteria.description: Editing a transaction amount updates that same table row, moves the affected summary cards, and updates the matching pie and sankey legend amounts without a reload

criteria.name: delete_flow_updates_all_surfaces
criteria.description: Deleting a transaction removes its row, drops it from selection, decreases the summary strip count by exactly one, and drops its amount from KPIs and chart legends

criteria.name: view_switch_retains_state
criteria.description: Switching Breakdown/Trends and focusing list versus chart keeps the transactions collection and filters intact without a page reload

criteria.name: last_delete_reveals_empty_state
criteria.description: After deleting all transactions, the list region shows an empty state and summary cards and strip read zero/empty

criteria.name: filters_update_all_surfaces
criteria.description: Category, income-vs-expense, date-range, and payee search filters narrow the table and recompute chart legends and summary tiles; clearing them restores the full list

criteria.name: collapsible_chrome_preserves_workflow
criteria.description: At narrow widths, any collapsed sidebar or overlay chrome can be reopened and the reports workflow continues without losing the transactions collection

criteria.name: overlays_support_expected_flows
criteria.description: Create/edit dialogs, the export drawer, import diagnostic panel, and command palette open, complete their actions, and dismiss without trapping the user

criteria.name: flow_recovers_without_reload
criteria.description: After dismissing an invalid create or closing the export drawer, the user can continue create/edit/filter flows without a page reload

criteria.name: export_flow_terminates_at_artifact
criteria.description: Export flow: after two creates with payees Ada Books and Evening Market, Export report JSON contains both payees, Markdown shows Expense Breakdown Report totals, and Download/Copy affordances work with a Copied confirmation

criteria.name: burn_rate_and_palette_flows
criteria.description: Burn rate flow: lowering the ceiling below projected month-end spend shows over-burn treatment; command palette flow: Cmd/Ctrl+K then Switch to Trends activates Trends

criteria.name: cold_start_is_under_two_seconds
criteria.description: The app is interactive within 2 seconds of a local cold load

criteria.name: console_is_clean
criteria.description: No console errors appear during a full exercise including create, edit, delete, filters, search, chart tabs, bulk actions, burn rate, command palette, export, and import

criteria.name: transitions_respond_under_100ms
criteria.description: Chart tab switches, filter changes, and export drawer opens apply without a perceptible multi-second freeze

criteria.name: async_work_has_loading_indicators
criteria.description: Import commit or export compile shows a busy indicator when work is not instantaneous

criteria.name: large_collections_render_without_lag
criteria.description: After importing or creating enough rows to roughly double the seed set, the table and charts remain scrollable and interactive

criteria.name: state_changes_remain_interactive
criteria.description: Rapid create/delete and filter toggles keep the UI responsive with no hung controls

criteria.name: animations_maintain_smooth_frame_rate
criteria.description: Row enter/exit and toast animations do not visibly stutter the rest of the reports chrome

criteria.name: rapid_input_does_not_freeze
criteria.description: Rapid repeated input on filters, search, and chart tabs does not freeze or drop interactions

criteria.name: consistent_capitalization_convention
criteria.description: Headings, buttons, and nav labels use one consistent capitalization convention throughout the app

criteria.name: consistent_currency_formatting
criteria.description: Currency values use the same symbol and decimal treatment across metric cards, table, legends, tooltips, summary strip, burn-rate panel, and export preview

criteria.name: toast_and_empty_state_copy_quality
criteria.description: Toasts name the action they confirm and empty states explain what belongs in the region and how to add it

criteria.name: export_drawer_and_over_burn_treatments
criteria.description: The export drawer shows JSON/Markdown tabs with a scrollable preview plus Download and Copy affordances; over-burn treatment uses an error color family distinct from ordinary row hover

criteria.name: no_browser_storage_seeded_reload
criteria.description: No browser storage is used for persistence: localStorage and sessionStorage remain empty after a full exercise of the app including export and import, and a reload returns the seeded state

criteria.name: console_clean_during_full_exercise
criteria.description: No console errors or unhandled promise rejections appear during a full exercise of the app (create, edit, delete, filters, search, chart tab switches, bulk actions, burn rate, command palette, export, import)

criteria.name: keyboard_operable_with_visible_focus
criteria.description: Every interactive control (sidebar items, chrome actions, chart tabs, table controls, form fields, Export report, Import CSV, Burn rate ceiling, command palette) is reachable and operable with the keyboard alone, and the focused control shows a visible focus indicator

criteria.name: modal_form_focus_trap_and_return
criteria.description: When the transaction create/edit form is presented as a modal it uses dialog semantics: focus moves into it on open, stays trapped inside while open, and returns to the invoking control on close

criteria.name: validation_announced_via_live_region
criteria.description: Form validation messages are exposed through a polite live region in addition to being shown visually

criteria.name: legends_convey_data_without_hover
criteria.description: Chart content is readable without hover: the legends list every expense category with its currency amount and percentage as plain text

criteria.name: interactive_within_two_seconds
criteria.description: The app is interactive within 2 seconds of a local cold load

criteria.name: in_place_redraws_without_jank
criteria.description: Chart redraws, tab switches, export drawer opens, and burn-rate edits apply in place without a full page reload and without visible jank

criteria.name: document_title_ledger_reports
criteria.description: The document title is exactly Ledger | Reports

criteria.name: no_outbound_requests_or_navigation
criteria.description: During a full exercise of the app, only same-origin network requests appear in devtools and no control navigates the browser to another origin

criteria.name: multi_facet_reload_resets_coherently
criteria.description: Configure several independent facets — create a transaction, switch the chart tab to Trends, apply a category filter, and change the burn-rate ceiling — then reload. All facets coherently reset to the seeded baseline, never a mix of reverted and surviving facets

criteria.name: export_drawer_and_palette_trap_focus
criteria.description: The export drawer and command palette trap focus while open, close on Escape, and return focus to the invoking control

criteria.name: field_contract_enforced_on_create
criteria.description: A create that violates the transaction field contract (zero amount, non-ISO date, or Salary with negative amount) is rejected with visible field errors and does not increase the collection count

criteria.name: layout_adapts_desktop_to_mobile
criteria.description: At 1024px and above the sidebar stays persistent beside the main column; at narrower widths the main column stacks vertically

criteria.name: mobile_tap_targets_are_large_enough
criteria.description: At 375px width, primary controls (chart tabs, Export report, Import CSV, row actions) remain large enough to activate without adjacent mis-taps

criteria.name: typography_resizes_across_breakpoints
criteria.description: Metric card values, table text, and panel headings remain readable across desktop and 375px widths

criteria.name: content_avoids_clipping_and_overflow
criteria.description: At narrower widths metric cards, chart card, burn-rate panel, table, and summary strip avoid clipping and page-level horizontal overflow

criteria.name: chrome_adapts_to_small_screens
criteria.description: Page chrome actions remain reachable at narrow widths, collapsing or wrapping rather than disappearing

criteria.name: stacking_reflows_logically
criteria.description: When stacked, cards, charts, burn-rate panel, and table appear in a readable top-to-bottom order

criteria.name: mobile_touch_gestures_work
criteria.description: At narrow widths, chart tab switches, filters, and row selection remain operable with tap interactions

criteria.name: small_screens_avoid_horizontal_scroll
criteria.description: At 375px width no page-level horizontal scrolling appears; the transactions table may scroll within its own container

criteria.name: media_and_canvases_resize
criteria.description: Sankey, doughnut, and burn-rate charts resize with their containers rather than overflowing at narrow widths

criteria.name: fixed_controls_remain_accessible
criteria.description: The export drawer, command palette, and import diagnostic panel stay fully visible and operable at 375px width rather than rendering off-screen

criteria.name: reduced_motion_disables_entry_exits
criteria.description: With prefers-reduced-motion set, entry/exit animations are removed and state changes apply instantly while export, import, and CRUD remain reachable

criteria.name: multi_facet_round_trip
criteria.description: Multi-facet round-trip: create a transaction, switch the chart tab to Trends, apply a category filter and a payee search query, then reload. All facets coherently reset to the seeded baseline — seeded transactions, default chart tab, cleared filters and search — never a mix of reverted and surviving state

criteria.name: sort_reversal_proves_live_data
criteria.description: Sort-reversal proof: sort the transactions table by amount ascending, note the order, then sort descending; the order reverses correctly relative to ascending, proving sort is derived from live data rather than two hardcoded orders

criteria.name: derived_view_responds_to_input
criteria.description: Derived-view sensitivity: change the category filter or date range and confirm the sankey/pie legends, summary strip, and burn-rate month-to-date change meaningfully rather than redrawing identically regardless of input

criteria.name: cross_view_echo_without_reload
criteria.description: Cross-view echo: create or edit a transaction in the table and confirm the same amount appears in the matching chart legend and in the Export report JSON totals without a reload

criteria.name: count_delta_is_exact
criteria.description: Count-delta integrity: measure the summary strip transaction count immediately before and immediately after creating one valid transaction; the delta is exactly +1 with no off-by-one or lagging counter

criteria.name: different_inputs_change_outcomes
criteria.description: Input-dependent output: create two expense transactions in different categories with different amounts; the two category legend lines and the Export report categoryBreakdown entries differ in the ways the inputs dictate

criteria.name: interleaved_flows_preserve_state
criteria.description: Interleaved-flow integrity: start creating a transaction, open the command palette and switch chart tabs, finish creating a second transaction, then confirm both rows exist and neither flow corrupted filters or burn-rate state

criteria.name: empty_to_repopulated_round_trip
criteria.description: Edge-state round-trip: delete all transactions so the empty state shows and KPIs read zero, then Import CSV with Replace all using valid rows; the table, cards, strip, charts, burn rate, and a fresh export track through both transitions

criteria.name: export_pipeline_contains_session_work
criteria.description: Full export pipeline: mutate the collection with two distinct payees, open Export report, and confirm the JSON transactions array contains both payees and totals.count matches the live strip; an export that omits either payee fails

criteria.name: import_export_round_trip_restores_state
criteria.description: Import/export round-trip: after mutations, capture the export JSON transaction set, delete all rows, Import CSV built from those rows with Replace all, and confirm the table and a fresh export transactions array match the captured set
